advantages: Recognizing underfitting early avoids wasting compute on huge datasets when a richer model or features are the real fix.
Spotting overfitting via train–val gaps prevents deploying models that look great in training dashboards but fail in production.
Validation curves give a repeatable recipe for picking complexity — less guesswork than tuning on training loss alone.
Early stopping and regularization triggered by overfitting signals save training time and stabilize serving behavior.
disadvantages: Train/val heuristics can mislead on tiny validation sets — noisy estimates may label a good model as overfit or underfit.
Some problems show high train and val error for reasons beyond capacity (bad labels, shift) — not every high/high pattern is underfitting.
Fixing overfitting with simpler models can reintroduce underfitting — tuning is iterative and dataset-specific.
Deep models may underfit optimization-wise while appearing overfit on a subset — diagnosis needs learning curves and multiple metrics.
